public_reputation: A scholar-administrator whose catalog memory is nearly punitive.
appearance: Powder-blue academic robes cut short for work, chain-bound stylus case, and silver page-mark sigils at the wrist.
manner: Corrects wording before content whenever both go wrong at once.
daily_function: Approves copied texts, enforces tariff schedules, and blocks unsanctioned circulation.
current_agenda: Identify the route by which restricted diagrams are leaving the Academy.
hidden_pressure: A leak from inside Mimolot would make the tariff regime look like theater.
first_impression: Archivist Temeris Quillward presents as Corrects wording before content whenever both go wrong at once. and immediately reads for leverage, obligation, and risk.
what_they_want_from_strangers: Useful clarity, respect for Tariff Archivist, and proof that strangers will not create avoidable noise in Principality of Mimolot.
what_they_refuse: Archivist Temeris Quillward refuses careless disrespect, vague promises, and demands that threaten standing or workload.
how_they_create_problems: By enforcing Tariff Archivist too rigidly, protecting reputation, or turning a small request into a larger obligation.
how_they_can_help: Archivist Temeris Quillward can open doors, explain local expectations, or redirect trouble before it reaches a harder authority layer.
escalation_if_crossed: If crossed, Archivist Temeris Quillward adds delay, scrutiny, and reputational drag before direct punishment.
rumor_about_them: People say Archivist Temeris Quillward is A scholar-administrator whose catalog memory is nearly punitive. and rarely acts without a second motive.
best_first_scene: A first encounter works best when the party needs guidance, access, or mercy from a Tariff Archivist.
